description: Courtney M. Cox — Hardwiring Hercules? (58 Ariz. St. L.J. 79 (2026))
show_notes:
Courtney M. Cox — Hardwiring Hercules? (58 Ariz. St. L.J. 79 (2026))

Courtney Cox reexamines the case for a right to a human decision in an age of automated decision-making. She targets Arguments from Explanation — the claim that machines should not replace human decision-makers because they cannot explain themselves — and argues that the hoped-for explanatory alignment between a decision and its justification is often an illusion, undermined by normative and moral uncertainty. Invoking Dworkin's ideal judge Hercules, she asks what it would actually take to hardwire sound judgment into a machine, and what the human-decision debate should focus on instead.
